1.7 We reiterated the following prior year’s recommendations and
management agreed to:

c. Replenish from its regular funds, the amount of ₽1,940,132.30

4.14 We reiterate the following prior year’s audit recommendations that
management:
a. Submit supporting expense receipts for claims of reimbursements
for communication expenses for CY 2015.
b. Make an analysis of actual official communication expenses
incurred by each official and department head as basis in
determining the levels of communication expenses to be granted
to ensure that such expenditures are within reasonable limits
and inure to the accomplishment of the objectives and mission of
the municipal government; and
c. Consider availing of mobile communication post-paid plans to
have a documentary trail of communication costs incurred by
each official entitled to reimbursement of communication
expense.
d. Cause the refund of the amounts claimed that were not or
insufficiently supported with expense receipts from the
concerned officials/employees

5.4 We recommended and management agreed to undertake following
courses of action:
5.4.1 Cause the refund by the contractors concerned of the
equivalent cost of the underruns in their specific projects
undertaken as computed above;
5.4.2 Closely monitor the scaling noted in the improvement of Kias
Barangay Road near Old Dynamite Bodega at Barangay
Virac, though these are considered minor. Require the
contractor to correct the same at his own expense, if they
worsen within the one year defects liability period. Also,
require the contractor to seal with asphalt the transversed
crack to prevent base saturation;
5.4.3 Ensure that contracts include provisions for liquidated
damages pursuant to Section 68 of RA No. 9184 as well as
contract time/duration for the project; and
5.4.4 Ensure that tarpaulin signboards of projects contain all the
data/information required therein. Signboards with
blank/incomplete project details shall be considered as non-
compliant and the pertinent cost thereof be deducted from
the project cost.

6.8 We recommended and management agreed to the following:

6.8.1 Request for the write-off of the loan receivables account
following the requirements and procedures cited in COA
Circular 2016-005 for the fair presentation of the assets in
the financial statements;

6.8.2 Until such time that the request for write-off is granted, it is
recommended that the loans receivables amounting to P
604,264.25 be reclassified to its appropriate accounts under
the non – current assets in the financial statements; and

6.8.3 Enforce the collection of the receivables and/or require the
refund of the outstanding cash advances from officials and
employees and the liquidation of fund transfers to the
barangays.

7.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to
review the Accounts Payable under the different funds and adjust
accordingly the balances of the account such that only those items
duly supported with IARs and supplier/creditor bills or payrolls as
the case maybe, shall be recognized as Accounts Payable.
Henceforth, entries for the recognition of liability account must be
fully supported and attached with inspection and acceptance reports
and creditor/supplier bills or invoices.

8.6 We recommended and management agreed to discontinue subjecting
to review and ratification by the SB, contracts for the procurement
of infrastructure projects that are specified in sufficient detail in
duly authorized annual or supplemental budgets and in the AIP.

10.6 We recommended and management agreed to require the members
of the GFPS, all elected officials and department heads to undergo
the capacity building programs on GAD as listed in Item 4.1.A.3 of
JMC No. 2013-01.

11.9 We recommended and management assented to the following:

11.9.1 Administer the HGDG test on its GAD PPAs, the results of
which shall be the basis in estimating the cost of gender
mainstreaming and the Gender and Development Budget.

11.9.2 Administer the HGDG test on its GAD PPAs during the
preparation of the GAD AR as basis in determining the
actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget
pursuant to JMC No. 2013-01.

12.6 We recommended that the GFP ensure the timely submission of the
GPB to the PPDO and then to the DILG for review and approval to
have a reasonable assurance that the GAD PPAs are responsive to
identified gender issues and concerns.

12.7 Management claimed that the GPB for CY 2016 hadbeen submitted
to DILG and a copy shall be furnish to the Audit Team.

13.3 We recommended and the Sanggunian Committee on Women and
Family or on Social Services agreed to spearhead the formulation of
the GAD Code in collaboration with the office departments of the
provincial government, national government agencies with local
presence.

14.4 We recommended and management agreed to establish a GAD
Database as a basis of planning, programming and policy
formulation in order to appropriately addressed existing gender
issues in the locality pursuant to JMC No. 2013-01.
